title: Expose coarse geo via server endpoint
summary: Create a Vercel serverless endpoint at /api/geo that reads coarse geolocation from Vercel's geolocation() helper and returns { country, region }. Create a client-side module to fetch this endpoint once at startup with graceful fallback to null values.

steps.title: Install @vercel/functions dependency
steps.description: Add @vercel/functions as a production dependency to access the geolocation() helper in the serverless function.
actions: Run: npm install @vercel/functions

steps.title: Create tsconfig for API functions
steps.description: The main tsconfig uses moduleResolution: bundler and noEmit: true, which won't work for Vercel serverless functions that need Node.js module resolution. Create a separate tsconfig.api.json that extends the base but overrides module settings for the api/ directory.
actions: Create tsconfig.api.json with target ES2023, module ESNext, moduleResolution node16, outDir .vercel/output, include ["api"]

steps.title: Create the /api/geo serverless endpoint
steps.description: Create api/geo.ts as a Vercel serverless function. Use geolocation() from @vercel/functions to read coarse geo headers (country, region) from the incoming request. Return JSON { country, region } with cache-control: no-store. Handle missing headers gracefully by returning null values.
actions: Import geolocation from @vercel/functions
Export default GET handler that reads geolocation(request)
Extract country and region (default to null if missing)
Return Response with JSON body { country, region }
Set headers: Content-Type application/json, Cache-Control no-store
Do NOT expose city, latitude, longitude, or any fine-grained data
acceptanceCriteria: Country header is read from Vercel environment
Region header is read from Vercel environment
Geo data is exposed via /api/geo endpoint
Endpoint returns only coarse values (country, region)

steps.title: Create the client-side geo fetch module
steps.description: Create src/input/geo.ts that exports a GeoHint interface and a fetchGeoHint() async function. It fetches /api/geo once, parses the JSON, and returns { country, region }. On any failure (network error, non-200, parse error), returns { country: null, region: null }.
actions: Define and export GeoHint interface: { country: string | null, region: string | null }
Export async function fetchGeoHint(): Promise<GeoHint>
Fetch /api/geo with no credentials
On success, parse JSON and return { country, region }
On any failure, return { country: null, region: null }
Do not access request headers directly from the client
acceptanceCriteria: Client does not access request headers directly
App works if geo data is unavailable

steps.title: Integrate geo fetch into application startup
steps.description: Update src/main.ts to call fetchGeoHint() at startup. The geo fetch should be fire-and-forget or awaited before scene init depending on whether geo data is needed for initial rendering. Since geo influences palette selection, fetch it early but don't block rendering.
actions: Import fetchGeoHint from ./input/geo
Call fetchGeoHint() at startup
Store result for use by session seed / visual mappings (future stories)
Do not block canvas rendering on geo fetch completion

steps.title: Write tests for the server endpoint
steps.description: Create tests/api/geo.test.ts to test the serverless function. Mock the geolocation() helper to return various values. Verify response shape, headers, and fallback behavior.
tests: Returns { country, region } when geolocation headers are present
Returns { country: null, region: null } when headers are missing
Does not expose city, latitude, longitude, or other fine-grained data
Returns partial data when only country is available
Returns partial data when only region is available

steps.title: Write tests for the client geo module
steps.description: Create tests/input/geo.test.ts to test fetchGeoHint(). Mock fetch to simulate success, failure, and malformed responses.
tests: Returns geo data on successful fetch
Returns { country: null, region: null } on network error
Returns { country: null, region: null } on non-200 response
Returns { country: null, region: null } on malformed JSON
Does not use localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies

steps.title: Verify build and privacy constraints
steps.description: Run the full build pipeline and existing test suite to ensure nothing is broken. Verify no forbidden storage APIs are introduced.
actions: Run: npm run build
Run: npm test
Verify no console errors in dev server
Confirm no localStorage/sessionStorage/cookie usage in new code

risks.risk: Vercel geolocation() returns empty/undefined in local dev
risks.mitigation: Client module already handles null fallback; server endpoint defaults to null for missing values. Local dev will simply return nulls.

risks.risk: tsconfig conflict between client (bundler resolution) and API (node resolution)
risks.mitigation: Separate tsconfig.api.json for the api/ directory; Vercel uses its own build pipeline for functions so the main tsconfig only needs to cover src/

definition_of_done: npm test passes (all new and existing tests)
No console errors on normal load
No forbidden storage APIs (cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage)
/api/geo returns { country, region } with cache-control: no-store
App renders correctly when geo endpoint is unavailable
